prompt: Which team did Wayne Gretzky famously get traded to from the Edmonton Oilers in 1988, shocking Canadian hockey fans?
explanation: Gretzky's 1988 trade to the Kings is often credited with boosting hockey's popularity in the United States.

prompt: Which goaltender holds the NHL record for most career wins?
explanation: Martin Brodeur's 691 career wins is the NHL record.

prompt: Which team did Martin Brodeur play the vast majority of his career for?
explanation: Brodeur spent 21 seasons with the New Jersey Devils, winning three Stanley Cups.

prompt: Which goaltender is known for winning four Stanley Cups and multiple Conn Smythe Trophies with the Montreal Canadiens and Colorado Avalanche?
explanation: Patrick Roy won titles with both Montreal and Colorado, earning legendary status as a clutch playoff performer.

prompt: Which trophy is awarded to the playoff MVP each year in the NHL?
explanation: The Conn Smythe Trophy is given to the most valuable player of the Stanley Cup playoffs.

prompt: Which trophy is given annually to the league's best defensive forward?
explanation: The Selke Trophy recognizes the forward who best excels in the defensive aspects of the game.

prompt: Which trophy is awarded to the NHL's best defenseman each season?
explanation: The Norris Trophy honors the league's top defenseman each year.

prompt: Which player holds the NHL record for most goals in a single season, scoring 92 in 1981-82?
explanation: Gretzky's 92-goal season remains the single-season NHL record decades later.

prompt: Which player holds the NHL record for most points in a single season, with 215 in 1985-86?
explanation: Gretzky's 215-point season is the most in a single NHL campaign.

prompt: Which Russian left winger became the second player ever to score 800 career NHL goals, playing his entire career with the Washington Capitals?
explanation: Alexander Ovechkin surpassed 800 career goals, chasing and later breaking Wayne Gretzky's all-time record.

prompt: Which player surpassed Wayne Gretzky's career goals record of 894 in 2024?
explanation: Ovechkin broke Gretzky's all-time NHL goals record during the 2024-25 season.

prompt: Which Pittsburgh Penguins captain has won two Stanley Cups and multiple Hart Trophies, often considered the best player of his generation?
explanation: Sidney Crosby has been a cornerstone of the Penguins franchise since being drafted first overall in 2005.

prompt: Which Edmonton Oilers center won back-to-back Hart Trophies in 2021 and 2022 and led the league in scoring multiple times?
explanation: Connor McDavid is widely regarded as the best player in the NHL today, known for his elite speed and playmaking.

prompt: Which German forward has been Connor McDavid's frequent linemate and a top scorer for the Edmonton Oilers?
explanation: Leon Draisaitl has formed one of the NHL's most dangerous scoring duos alongside McDavid.

prompt: Which NHL franchise is nicknamed the 'Original Six' along with the Bruins, Blackhawks, Red Wings, Rangers, and Maple Leafs?
explanation: The Original Six refers to the NHL's six teams from 1942 to 1967, before league expansion began.

prompt: Which expansion era added the most teams at once, dramatically doubling the NHL's size in 1967?
options: The 1967 expansion added six teams, doubling the league to 12
explanation: The 1967 expansion doubled the NHL from six to twelve teams in a single offseason.

prompt: Which four teams joined the NHL in 1979 following the collapse of the rival World Hockey Association (WHA)?
options: Edmonton Oilers, Hartford Whalers, Quebec Nordiques, Winnipeg Jets
Colorado Avalanche, Dallas Stars, Arizona Coyotes, Carolina Hurricanes
Vegas Golden Knights, Seattle Kraken, San Jose Sharks, Anaheim Ducks
Ottawa Senators, Tampa Bay Lightning, Florida Panthers, San Jose Sharks
explanation: These four WHA teams joined the NHL in the 1979 merger, adding stars like Wayne Gretzky to the league.

prompt: Which team relocated from Winnipeg to Phoenix (later Arizona) in 1996, before a new Winnipeg Jets franchise was formed in 2011?
options: The original Winnipeg Jets became the Phoenix Coyotes
The Quebec Nordiques became the Coyotes
The Hartford Whalers became the Coyotes
The Minnesota North Stars became the Coyotes
explanation: The original Winnipeg Jets relocated in 1996, and Atlanta's Thrashers later moved back to Winnipeg in 2011 as the new Jets.

prompt: Which team is the most recent NHL expansion franchise, joining the league in 2021?
explanation: The Seattle Kraken began play in the 2021-22 season as the NHL's 32nd franchise.

prompt: Which expansion team reached the Stanley Cup Final in its very first season in 2018, an unprecedented feat?
explanation: The Vegas Golden Knights' remarkable inaugural-season run to the Final was one of the most surprising stories in NHL history.

prompt: Which Chicago Blackhawks star won three Stanley Cups (2010, 2013, 2015) and an Olympic gold medal, cementing a Hall of Fame career?
explanation: Jonathan Toews captained the Blackhawks to three championships during their 2010s dynasty run.

prompt: Which Chicago Blackhawks forward won the Conn Smythe Trophy as playoff MVP in 2013?
explanation: Patrick Kane was named playoff MVP after leading Chicago to the 2013 Stanley Cup.

prompt: Which Pittsburgh Penguins Russian forward has won the Hart Trophy and multiple scoring titles alongside Sidney Crosby?
explanation: Evgeni Malkin has been a dominant offensive force for Pittsburgh since being drafted in 2004.

prompt: Which Tampa Bay Lightning Russian winger won the Art Ross Trophy and Hart Trophy in the same season, in 2018-19?
explanation: Kucherov's dominant 2018-19 season made him one of the league's premier offensive talents.

prompt: Which Tampa Bay Lightning captain and sniper has led the team in scoring for much of his career and won two Stanley Cups?
explanation: Steven Stamkos has been a franchise cornerstone for Tampa Bay since being drafted first overall in 2008.

prompt: Which Swedish defenseman won the Norris Trophy and helped lead the Tampa Bay Lightning to back-to-back Stanley Cups in 2020 and 2021?
explanation: Victor Hedman has been one of the league's premier two-way defensemen since his career began.

prompt: Which Swedish defenseman is widely regarded as one of the best defensemen of the 2000s and 2010s, winning the Norris Trophy multiple times with Detroit?
explanation: Nicklas Lidstrom won seven Norris Trophies and multiple Stanley Cups with the Detroit Red Wings.

prompt: Which team's 'Legion of Doom' line in the 1990s featured Eric Lindros, John LeClair, and Mikael Renberg?
explanation: The 'Legion of Doom' line was one of the most feared offensive units of the 1990s NHL.

prompt: Which New York Rangers captain ended the franchise's 54-year Stanley Cup drought in 1994?
explanation: Mark Messier's leadership and famous 'guarantee' helped the Rangers win their first title since 1940.

prompt: How many Stanley Cups did Mark Messier win over his career, split between Edmonton and New York?
explanation: Messier won five titles with Edmonton and one with the Rangers, for a total of six championships.

prompt: Which Colorado Avalanche captain led the team to Stanley Cup titles in 1996 and 2001?
explanation: Joe Sakic's leadership and playmaking made him one of the greatest Avalanche players ever.

prompt: Which longtime Boston Bruins defenseman finally won his first Stanley Cup with the Colorado Avalanche at the end of his career in 2001?
explanation: Ray Bourque's emotional championship in his final NHL season capped off a legendary career.

prompt: Which Boston Bruins captain led the team to a Stanley Cup title in 2011, ending a long championship drought?
explanation: Zdeno Chara captained the Bruins to their first title since 1972 in 2011.

prompt: Which Boston Bruins forward is widely considered one of the best two-way (defensive and offensive) centers of his generation, winning multiple Selke Trophies?
explanation: Bergeron won a record-tying six Selke Trophies for his defensive excellence at center.

prompt: Which Chicago Blackhawks defenseman won the Norris Trophy multiple times and was a key part of the 2010s dynasty?
explanation: Duncan Keith's elite two-way play earned him multiple Norris Trophies and Olympic gold medals.

prompt: Which St. Louis Blues franchise finally won its first Stanley Cup in franchise history in 2019?
explanation: The Blues won their first title in 2019 after being founded in 1967, capping an emotional postseason run.

prompt: Which team has the longest active Stanley Cup drought as of the mid-2020s, having never won since the Original Six era ended?
explanation: The Toronto Maple Leafs' championship drought, dating to 1967, is the longest in the NHL.

prompt: Unlike forwards, who have separate awards for offense (Art Ross) and defense (Selke), which single trophy covers all-around excellence for defensemen?
explanation: The Norris Trophy is the sole major individual award recognizing top defensemen, covering both ends of the ice.

prompt: Which award recognizes a player's leadership qualities and humanitarian contributions off the ice?
explanation: The King Clancy Trophy honors leadership on the ice and humanitarian work in the community.

prompt: Which Detroit Red Wings captain led the team to Stanley Cups in 1997, 1998, and 2002?
explanation: Steve Yzerman's leadership as captain helped end Detroit's long championship drought in the 1990s.

prompt: Which Russian forward, along with Yzerman, was a key offensive star for Detroit's championship teams in the late 1990s?
explanation: Sergei Fedorov was a dominant two-way forward for Detroit's championship-era teams.

prompt: Which Russian playmaking center known for his creative passing played for Detroit in the 2000s and helped win the 2002 and 2008 Cups?
explanation: Pavel Datsyuk was renowned for his elite stickhandling and playmaking with the Red Wings.

prompt: Which Los Angeles Kings franchise won their first Stanley Cup titles in franchise history in 2012 and 2014?
explanation: The Kings won their first two championships in these seasons, ending decades without a title.

prompt: Which Anaheim Ducks franchise won its only Stanley Cup title in 2007?
explanation: The Ducks, led by Scott Niedermayer and Chris Pronger, won their first championship in 2007.

prompt: Which Carolina Hurricanes franchise, relocated from Hartford, won its only Stanley Cup in 2006?
explanation: The Hurricanes, who relocated from Hartford in 1997, won their first and only title in 2006.

prompt: Which team won the Stanley Cup in 2022?
explanation: The Colorado Avalanche defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning to win the 2022 title.

prompt: Which team won the Stanley Cup in 2023, its first championship since joining the league in 2017?
explanation: Vegas won its first title in just its sixth season of existence.

prompt: Which Florida Panthers franchise won its first Stanley Cup title in 2024?
explanation: The Panthers won their first championship in franchise history in 2024, then repeated in 2025.

statement: Martin Brodeur holds the NHL record for most career wins by a goaltender.
explanation: Brodeur's 691 wins remains the all-time record.

statement: Wayne Gretzky's single-season record of 92 goals was set during the 1981-82 season.
explanation: This remains the NHL's single-season goal-scoring record.

statement: Alexander Ovechkin surpassed Wayne Gretzky's all-time career goals record during the 2024-25 season.
explanation: Ovechkin's record-breaking goal made him the NHL's new all-time leading goal scorer.

statement: The Vegas Golden Knights reached the Stanley Cup Final in their very first season, in 2018.
explanation: Their inaugural run was one of the most surprising stories in NHL history.

statement: The St. Louis Blues won their first Stanley Cup in franchise history in 2019.
explanation: The Blues had never won a title before their emotional 2019 championship run.

statement: The Toronto Maple Leafs have the longest active Stanley Cup championship drought in the NHL.
explanation: Toronto's last title came in 1967, the longest drought among current NHL franchises.

statement: Mark Messier won six Stanley Cups across his career with Edmonton and New York.
explanation: Messier won five with the Oilers and one with the Rangers.

statement: Patrick Roy won Stanley Cups with both the Montreal Canadiens and the Colorado Avalanche.
explanation: Roy's championship success with two different franchises is part of his Hall of Fame legacy.

statement: The Florida Panthers won their first Stanley Cup title in franchise history in 2024.
explanation: The Panthers won their inaugural championship in 2024 after previously losing in the 2023 Final.

statement: The 1967 NHL expansion doubled the league from six to twelve teams.
explanation: This was the largest single expansion in NHL history relative to league size at the time.

explanation: Crosby has been the face of the NHL for nearly two decades since his debut.

clues: Playing for the Washington Capitals his entire career
Surpassed Wayne Gretzky's all-time goals record in 2024-25
explanation: Ovechkin's goal-scoring prowess made him the NHL's new all-time leader in career goals.

Known for exceptional speed and playmaking

prompt: Order these NHL expansion and franchise events chronologically (earliest first).
events: The NHL expands from six to twelve teams (1967)
Four WHA teams join the NHL, including the Edmonton Oilers (1979)
The Vegas Golden Knights join as an expansion team (2017)
The Seattle Kraken join as an expansion team (2021)
explanation: Order: 1967 expansion, 1979 WHA merger, Vegas 2017, Seattle 2021.

prompt: Order these Stanley Cup droughts ending, chronologically (earliest first).
events: New York Rangers end a 54-year drought (1994)
Detroit Red Wings end a long drought under Steve Yzerman (1997)
St. Louis Blues win their first title in franchise history (2019)
Florida Panthers win their first title in franchise history (2024)
explanation: Order: Rangers 1994, Red Wings 1997, Blues 2019, Panthers 2024.

prompt: Which Pittsburgh Penguins Czech forward was known as one of the highest-paid and most dominant players of the 1990s?
explanation: Jaromir Jagr won multiple scoring titles and two Stanley Cups with Pittsburgh alongside Mario Lemieux.

prompt: Which player holds the NHL record for most career games played?
explanation: Patrick Marleau surpassed Gordie Howe's long-standing record for most career games played.

prompt: Which goaltender recorded the most career shutouts in NHL history?
explanation: Brodeur's 125 career shutouts is the NHL record.

prompt: Which Czech goaltender won back-to-back Vezina Trophies and was famous for his unorthodox style with Buffalo?
explanation: Dominik Hasek, nicknamed 'The Dominator', won six Vezina Trophies during his career.

prompt: Which Finnish forward known as a prolific playoff scorer helped the Pittsburgh Penguins win Cups in the early 1990s?
explanation: Jari Kurri, formerly Wayne Gretzky's linemate in Edmonton, later won a Cup with Pittsburgh in 1992.

prompt: Which Finnish forward, nicknamed 'The Finnish Flash', set the NHL rookie goal-scoring record with 76 goals in 1992-93?
explanation: Teemu Selanne's rookie record of 76 goals for the Winnipeg Jets still stands today.

prompt: Which Colorado Avalanche forward has emerged as one of the league's top scorers in the early 2020s?
explanation: Nathan MacKinnon has developed into one of the NHL's premier offensive centers with the Avalanche.

prompt: Which Colorado Avalanche defenseman won the Norris Trophy and Conn Smythe Trophy in the same season (2022)?
explanation: Cale Makar's dominant two-way play helped Colorado win the 2022 Stanley Cup.

prompt: Which New Jersey Devils defensive system, built around stifling neutral-zone play, helped the team win three Cups between 1995 and 2003?
explanation: The Devils' trap system frustrated high-scoring opponents throughout the late 1990s and early 2000s.

prompt: Which Detroit Red Wings defenseman won the Norris Trophy a record-tying seven times?
explanation: Lidstrom's seven Norris Trophies ties Bobby Orr and Doug Harvey for the most all-time.

prompt: Which Ottawa Senators and San Jose Sharks defenseman is a multiple-time Norris Trophy winner known for his offensive rushes from the blue line?
explanation: Erik Karlsson's dynamic offensive play from the back end has earned him multiple Norris Trophies.

prompt: Which player scored the 'goal heard around the world' to win Olympic gold for Canada in overtime in 2010?
explanation: Crosby's overtime golden goal against the USA gave Canada gold on home ice in Vancouver.

prompt: Which country won men's Olympic hockey gold at the 2010 Vancouver Games?
explanation: Canada defeated the USA 3-2 in overtime on home ice to win gold.

prompt: Which country won men's Olympic hockey gold at the 2014 Sochi Games?
explanation: Canada defended their title, beating Sweden 3-0 in the final.

prompt: Which Buffalo Sabres winger became the first Russian-trained player to captain an NHL team and won the Lady Byng Trophy twice?
explanation: Alexander Mogilny had a decorated career with the Sabres, Devils, and Maple Leafs, winning a Stanley Cup with New Jersey in 2000.

prompt: Which Vancouver Canucks winger, nicknamed 'The Russian Rocket', was known for his blazing speed and highlight-reel goals in the 1990s?
explanation: Pavel Bure's speed and scoring touch made him one of the most electrifying players of the decade.

prompt: Which award is given to the coach judged to have contributed the most to his team's success each season?
options: Coach of the Year Award (informal name only)
explanation: The Jack Adams Award recognizes the NHL's top head coach each season.

prompt: Which coach won the most Stanley Cups in NHL history, primarily with the Montreal Canadiens dynasty of the 1970s?
explanation: Scotty Bowman won nine Stanley Cups as a head coach, the most in NHL history.

prompt: Which coach led the Montreal Canadiens dynasty of the 1950s and 1960s, winning eight Stanley Cups?
explanation: Toe Blake's eight championships as Montreal's coach remain among the most in league history.

prompt: Which New York Islanders coach led the team to four consecutive Stanley Cups from 1980 to 1983?
explanation: Al Arbour guided the Islanders dynasty to a run of four straight championships.

prompt: Which Edmonton Oilers coach led Wayne Gretzky's teams to five Stanley Cups in the 1980s?
explanation: Glen Sather built and coached the Oilers dynasty that dominated the mid-to-late 1980s.

prompt: Which team's rivalry with the Montreal Canadiens is often called the oldest and most storied rivalry in the NHL?
explanation: The Canadiens-Maple Leafs rivalry dates back to the earliest days of the NHL.

prompt: Which trophy is awarded to the top NHL team in the regular season standings each year?
explanation: The Presidents' Trophy goes to the team with the best regular-season record.

prompt: Which conference trophy is awarded to the winner of the Eastern Conference finals?
explanation: The Prince of Wales Trophy goes to the NHL's Eastern Conference playoff champion.

prompt: Which conference trophy is awarded to the winner of the Western Conference finals?
explanation: The Clarence Campbell Bowl goes to the NHL's Western Conference playoff champion.

prompt: Which Washington Capitals goaltender helped the team win its first Stanley Cup title in 2018?
explanation: Braden Holtby was the Capitals' starting goaltender during their historic 2018 championship run.

prompt: Which Washington Capitals captain finally won his first Stanley Cup in 2018 after over a decade with the franchise?
explanation: Ovechkin's 2018 championship silenced critics who questioned whether he could win a title.

prompt: Which St. Louis Blues goaltender, an unlikely midseason waiver-wire pickup, led the team to the 2019 Stanley Cup and won the Conn Smythe Trophy?
explanation: Binnington's surprising emergence midseason was a key storyline in the Blues' improbable title run.

prompt: Which Tampa Bay Lightning goaltender won back-to-back Stanley Cups in 2020 and 2021?
explanation: Vasilevskiy has established himself as one of the league's top goaltenders during Tampa's championship era.

prompt: Which Vegas Golden Knights goaltender was famous for his 'Fear the Fribble' antics and became a fan favorite during the team's inaugural 2018 playoff run?
explanation: Marc-Andre Fleury's stellar play helped drive Vegas's unprecedented run to the 2018 Stanley Cup Final.

prompt: Which team did Marc-Andre Fleury spend the first 13 seasons of his career with, winning three Stanley Cups?
explanation: Fleury won championships with Pittsburgh in 2009, 2016, and 2017 before joining Vegas.

explanation: Marleau broke Howe's long-standing record during the 2018-19 season.

explanation: Bowman's nine championships as a coach is an NHL record.

explanation: This ended years of playoff disappointment for Ovechkin and the Capitals.

statement: Jordan Binnington was a longtime starting goaltender for the Blues before their 2019 championship season.
explanation: Binnington was a surprising midseason call-up who became the starter during the Blues' title run.

statement: Teemu Selanne's rookie record of 76 goals in a single season still stands.
explanation: No rookie has come close to matching Selanne's 1992-93 goal total since.

statement: The New Jersey Devils' 'Neutral Zone Trap' system helped them win three Stanley Cups between 1995 and 2003.
explanation: This defensive system became notorious for stifling high-powered offenses in that era.

explanation: Makar's dominant two-way performance helped Colorado capture the Stanley Cup that year.

explanation: Fleury was Pittsburgh's starting goaltender for titles in 2009, 2016, and 2017.

explanation: It recognizes the coach judged to have contributed the most to his team's success.

prompt: Order these individual NHL award-related milestones chronologically (earliest first).
events: Toe Blake wins his eighth Stanley Cup as Montreal's coach (1968)
Wayne Gretzky sets the single-season points record with 215 (1985-86)
Teemu Selanne sets the rookie goal record with 76 (1992-93)
Cale Makar wins the Norris and Conn Smythe Trophies in the same season (2022)
